senses.definition: things that are related to air and sky such as temperature, rain, wind, etc.

examples.x: The weather in this city is usually mild throughout the year.
The weather is sunny and warm today, perfect for a picnic.
The weather report says there will be snowfall tomorrow.
We had to cancel our outdoor plans due to the stormy weather.

senses.definition: the result of something burning that often produces heat, flame, light, and smoke

examples.x: Can you please add some logs to the fire to keep it burning?
The firefighter bravely extinguished the house fire.
We enjoyed sitting by the fire, telling stories and staying warm.
We used a lighter to start the fire in the fireplace.

senses.definition: having a higher than normal temperature

examples.x: I turned on the air conditioner because it was getting too hot inside.
She wore a hat to protect herself from the hot sun.
The hot water in the shower helped me relax after a long day.
The metal handle became hot when left in the sun.
The soup was too hot to eat right away.

senses.definition: having a temperature lower than the human body's average temperature

examples.x: I wore a warm coat to protect myself from the cold wind.
The air conditioning made the room too cold, so I adjusted the temperature.
The ice cubes made the drink refreshingly cold.

senses.definition: very bright because there is a lot of light coming from the sun

examples.x: It's a beautiful sunny day, perfect for a picnic in the park.
The flowers bloomed under the sunny sky.
The sunny day encouraged us to go for a bike ride.
The sunny weather makes me feel happy and energized.
The sunny weather melted the snow, revealing patches of green grass.

senses.definition: a white or gray visible mass of water vapor floating in the air

examples.x: My little son was looking up at the sky and showing me the fluffy white clouds.
The airplane flew through the clouds on its way to the destination.
The clouds blocked the sun, creating a momentary shadow.
The dark clouds signaled that rain was on its way.
We sat under a tree, watching the clouds slowly drift across the sky.

senses.definition: having many clouds up in the sky

examples.x: I carried an umbrella with me because the weather looked cloudy.
I couldn't see any stars because it was too cloudy at night.
The cloudy sky created a dramatic backdrop for the sunset.
The weather forecast predicted a cloudy morning followed by clearing in the afternoon.
We decided to postpone our outdoor plans due to the cloudy weather.

senses.definition: water that falls in small drops from the sky

examples.x: Can you hear the sound of rain tapping on the window?
I forgot my umbrella, and now I'm getting wet in the rain.
The rain made the grass and flowers look vibrant and alive.
The rain stopped, and a rainbow appeared in the sky.
The rain washed away the dust and made everything fresh and clean.

senses.definition: having frequent or persistent rainfall

examples.x: I like to listen to the sound of raindrops falling on the roof during a rainy day.
I stayed indoors because it was a rainy day.
The rainy afternoon was perfect for staying in and reading a book.
The rainy weather made the streets slippery.

senses.definition: ‌(of a period of time or weather) having or bringing snow

examples.x: We had a snowy weekend in the mountains, surrounded by beautiful white landscapes.
Despite the snowy conditions, the adventurous group went on a hiking expedition in the mountains.
The forecast warns of a snowy week ahead, so residents should be careful on the roads.
Families gathered for a snowy picnic, savoring the chance to enjoy the outdoors in the winter chill.
He slipped on the snowy sidewalk while rushing to catch the bus.

senses.definition: frozen water, which has a solid state

examples.x: I slipped and fell on the ice while walking on the frozen lake.
The hockey players glided effortlessly on the ice.
The windshield was covered in ice, so I had to scrape it before driving.

senses.definition: everything that exists or happens on the earth, excluding things that humans make or control

examples.x: Can you hear the sound of nature?
I find solace in sitting by the river, listening to the soothing sounds of nature.
I love spending time in nature, surrounded by trees, flowers, and fresh air.
The beauty of nature is evident in the vibrant colors of a sunset.
The changing seasons offer a variety of experiences and beauty in nature.

senses.definition: the large, bright star in the sky that shines during the day and gives us light and heat

examples.x: The sun shines brightly, lighting up the entire sky.
I love watching the sunset as the sun paints the sky with vibrant colors.
I wear sunglasses to protect my eyes from the sun's glare.
The sun rises in the east and sets in the west.
The sunflower turned its face towards the sun.

senses.definition: the circular object going round the earth, visible mostly at night

examples.x: Can you see the moon peeking out from behind the clouds?
I enjoy watching the moon rise above the horizon.
Selenophiles have a deep and intense love for the moon.
The moon looked so close, as if we could reach out and touch it.

senses.definition: a big round mass covered in land and water, on which we all live
the space above the earth where the sun, clouds, stars, and the moon are and we can see them

examples.x: The dark clouds covered the sky, signaling an approaching storm.
I love lying on the grass and watching the clouds float across the sky.
The sky gradually brightened as the morning sun rose.
The sky seemed endless, stretching as far as the eye could see.
The sky turned pink and orange as the sun began to set.

senses.definition: a natural and continuous stream of water flowing on the land to the sea, a lake, or another river

examples.x: I dipped my feet in the cool water of the river.
The river flowed gently, reflecting the surrounding trees.
The river was wide, and we had to cross it using a bridge.
We went fishing by the river and caught some fresh trout.

senses.definition: the salt water that covers most of the earth’s surface and surrounds its continents and islands

examples.x: I hear the sound of seagulls near the sea.
The sea is home to a variety of marine life, such as fish, dolphins, and coral reefs.
The sea was calm, and the waves gently lapped against the shore.
We collected seashells while walking along the edge of the sea.
We spent our vacation relaxing on the sandy beaches by the sea.

senses.definition: a very tall and large natural structure that looks like a huge hill with a pointed top that is often covered in snow

examples.x: The mountain is a popular destination for climbers and hikers.
The mountain provided a natural barrier between the two valleys.
We enjoyed a picnic at the base of the mountain, surrounded by nature.
We hiked up the mountain and enjoyed the breathtaking view from the top.

senses.definition: an area of sand or small stones next to a sea or a lake

examples.x: I buried my feet in the warm sand at the beach.
I love strolling along the beach, feeling the soft sand between my toes.
The beach is a great place to relax and unwind during vacation.
The beach was crowded with people enjoying the sun and surf.
We had a picnic on the sandy beach, enjoying the ocean breeze.

senses.definition: a vast area of land that is covered with trees and shrubs

examples.x: I love the fresh scent of pine trees in the forest.
The forest floor was covered in a thick carpet of fallen leaves.
The forest is home to a diverse range of plant and animal species.
The forest provided us with shade on a hot summer day.
We went for a walk in the forest, surrounded by tall trees and chirping birds.

senses.definition: a piece of land surrounded by water

examples.x: I collected seashells as souvenirs from the beautiful island.
The island had a famous lighthouse that guided ships safely to shore.
The island had stunning sunsets that painted the sky with vibrant hues.
We took a boat to the tropical island and spent the day lounging on the sandy beach.
We witnessed sea turtles nesting on the shores of the island.

senses.definition: (astronomy) a shining point found in large numbers in the night sky

examples.x: The brightest star in the night sky is Sirius, also known as the Dog Star.
The sky was so clear that I could see countless stars.
The stars twinkled brightly in the dark night sky.
We used a telescope to observe distant stars and galaxies.

senses.definition: a very tall plant with branches and leaves, that can live a long time

examples.x: The leaves on the tree turned vibrant shades of red, orange, and yellow in the autumn.
The tree was home to a family of birds, who built their nest among the branches.
We climbed the sturdy branches of the tree to get a better view.

senses.definition: a part of a plant from which the seed or fruit develops

examples.x: I decided to buy myself a bouquet of flowers to brighten up my home.
I picked a bouquet of fresh flowers from the field.
The petals of the flower were delicate and soft to the touch.
We decorated the table with a vase of freshly flowers.
We planted seeds and watched as the flowers grew.
